text: Activities that compose the practical classes are undoubtedly one of the most important methodological resources for the teaching and learning process of science, since they arouse the interest of students for scientific phenomena, allowing the construction of knowledge and student critical thinking regarding the surrounding world. However, the lectures are usually considered more important. Therefore, this study aimed to evaluate, characterize and encourage the practical classes in Science Education in two schools of Vitória de Santo Antão / PE, one located in the Rural Area (Duque de Caxias) and the other in the Urban Area (August 3). The data were collected during field visits and through questionnaires applied to teachers from the schools involved in the research. The results show that the Duque de Caxias School has better conditions and more involvement of teachers in the implementation of practical classes in Science Education when compared to the August 3 School. The main reasons given by the teachers of the two schools for the low implementation of practical classes in Science Education were lack of infrastructure, high number of students in the classes and lack of availability of educators. Based on this basis, the workbooks were developed for students of 6th to 9th grades of elementary school in order to assist teachers in the teaching and learning process.
